_comment: Engaged-soldier highlight demo (Settings.show_engaged_highlight, dev/debug visual): reuses anti-cav-square.json's rear-cavalry-charge setup (a lone player Infantry unit squares up against two cavalry charging its rear) as its own copy rather than editing the shared anti-cav-square.json in place, since that file is also reused by demo.380.json and website/tools/record-demos.sh's 'square' entry -- turning on a debug overlay there would change what those unrelated demos show. Requests the highlight via the session-only 'show_engaged_highlight' script field (Settings.set_show_engaged_highlight_session, DemoInputRecorder.gd), never persisted. Unlike the original anti-cav-square.json, this ALSO issues Ctrl+H (Hold, immediate via SelectionManager._issue_stance) right after selecting the unit -- the original scenario leaves it on the default Normal order, which auto-chases any detected enemy (Unit._think's default behaviour) and drags the block's centroid noticeably toward the cavalry over the clip, an unrelated movement artifact next to the thing this demo actually wants to show. Held in place, the block receives the charge stationary (still fights once contact is made -- HOLD only suppresses the auto-chase, per test_hold_unit_still_fights_an_enemy_in_contact), which isolates the amber highlight tracking the LIVE ring from that drift. The clip runs well past the point casualties start (~tick 240 in the underlying scenario), so the highlight is shown tracking the ring as soldiers die and the per-soldier array compacts -- the exact case UnitFormation.live_perimeter_indices fixes.
